properties.name: Advice
properties.location: Ovens River downstream of Rocky Point
properties.webBody:
This Minor Flood Warning is being issued for the Ovens River downstream of Rocky Point.

Minor flooding is possible at Wangaratta overnight Wednesday into Thursday.

Rainfall across the Ovens and King River catchments since Saturday afternoon has resulted in renewed river level rises across the catchments. Minor flooding peaked along the King River at Docker Road Bridge Tuesday evening. Combined flows from the Ovens and King rivers may cause a minor flood peak at Wangaratta overnight Wednesday into Thursday.

Ovens River downstream of Rocky Point:

Minor flooding is possible along the Ovens River at Wangaratta. The Ovens River at Wangaratta was at 11.49 m and steady at 8:00 pm Tuesday, below the minor flood level. The Ovens River at Wangaratta may peak near the minor flood level (11.90 m) overnight Wednesday into Thursday.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do: 
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings.
  • Farmers should consider moving livestock and machinery to higher ground.
  • Contact family members and neighbours to ensure they are aware of the situation.
Impacts in your area: 
  • High river levels and potential flooding in areas close to rivers and streams.
  • Caravan parks and camping grounds may be flooded.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 16/07/2026 12:00 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility: 
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. 
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline. 
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.name: Advice
properties.location: Kiewa River downstream of Mongans Bridge
properties.webBody:
This Minor Flood Warning is being issued for the Kiewa River downstream of Mongans Bridge.

Minor flooding continuing at Bandiana for next few days, possible at Kiewa from Wednesday afternoon.

Minor flooding continues along the Kiewa River at Bandiana, where the river level is expected to remain above the minor flood level over the next few days. Further upstream, renewed rises are occurring along the Kiewa River Channel at Kiewa which may peak near the minor flood level from Wednesday afternoon. No significant rainfall is forecast for the next few days.

Kiewa River downstream of Mongans Bridge:

Minor flooding is continuing at Bandiana and possible at Kiewa. 

The Kiewa River Channel at Kiewa is currently at 3.24 m and rising, below the minor flood level. The Kiewa River Channel at Kiewa may peak near the minor flood level (3.30 m) from Wednesday afternoon. 

The Kiewa River at Bandiana is currently at 2.89 m and steady, with minor flooding. The Kiewa River at Bandiana is expected to remain above the minor flood level (2.80 m) over the next few days.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do: 
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings.
  • Contact family members and neighbours to ensure they are aware of the situation.
  • Farmers should consider moving livestock and machinery to higher ground.
Impacts in your area: 
  • High river levels and potential flooding in areas close to rivers and streams.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 16/07/2026 12:30 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility: 
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. 
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline. 
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.name: Advice
properties.webBody:
This Minor Flood Warning is being issued for the King River.

Minor flooding occurring at Docker Road bridge and possible at Wangaratta overnight Wednesday into Thursday.

Rainfall across the Ovens and King River catchments since Saturday afternoon has resulted in renewed river level rises across the catchments. Minor flooding peaked along the King River at Docker Road Bridge Tuesday evening. Combined flows from the Ovens and King rivers may cause a minor flood peak at Wangaratta overnight Wednesday into Thursday.

King River:

Minor flooding is occurring along the King River at Docker Road Bridge. The King River at Docker Rd Bridge peaked at 3.81 m around 09:45 pm Tuesday, with minor flooding. The river is currently at 3.79 m and steady, with minor flooding. The King River at Docker Rd Bridge may fall below the minor flood level (3.70 m) from Wednesday evening.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do: 
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings. 
  • Farmers should consider moving livestock and machinery to higher ground.
  • Contact family members and neighbours to ensure they are aware of the situation.
Impacts in your area: 
  • High river levels and potential flooding in areas close to rivers and streams.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 16/07/2026 12:00 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility: 
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. 
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline. 
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.name: Advice
properties.webBody:
This is a Final Minor Flood Warning for the Seven Creeks to Euroa. 

Minor flooding no longer occurring at Euroa.

Minor flooding has eased along the Seven Creeks at Euroa. No significant rainfall is forecast for the next few days and no further flooding is expected.

Seven Creeks to Euroa:

Minor flooding has eased at Euroa.

The Seven Creeks at Euroa is currently at 2.34 m and steady, below the minor flood level. The Seven Creeks at Euroa is expected to remain below the minor flood level (2.50 m).
The threat of flooding is reduced. Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and beware of hazards remaining in the warning area.
What you should do:
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes.
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never enter floodwater.
If your home or business has been flooded:
  • Check for building damage before you enter.
  • Have all electrical and gas equipment professionally tested before use.
  • Clean and dry out the building as soon as possible.
Call Triple Zero (000) in a life threatening emergency. For emergency assistance from the SES call 132 500.
Impacts in your area: 
  • Damage to roads and buildings caused by flooding may still exist in your area. 
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

This will be the last warning for this flood. 

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility:
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency.
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.
